articles.title: Geo News taken off air for 15 days as Pakistan cracks down on Muharram broadcast over religious concerns
articles.description: Pakistan has suspended Geo News for 15 days over a Muharram broadcast, citing religious sensitivities and risks to public order. The move has renewed concerns over media censorship and sectarian tensions in the country.

Vir Das defends Kangana Ranaut over 'Revolver Rani' kissing rumour
articles.description: Vir Das denied rumours of being injured by Kangana Ranaut during a kissing scene in 'Revolver Rani'. He called the story 'pure fiction' and Kangana a 'complete professional'. Kangana also reacted, dismissing the viral claim on social media.

articles.title: Shiv Sena warns Kharge over RSS remarks, 'happiness won't last long'

articles.title: Adani case: US court seeks DOJ clarification on dropping charges
articles.description: A US court has sought clarification from the Department of Justice on its motion to drop charges against Gautam and Sagar Adani. US lawyer Adam Goldberg says this is a routine procedure and the judge is likely to approve the DOJ's request.

articles.title: Ayodhya temple fund case: Ex-Trust official Champat Rai questioned
articles.description: Police have recorded the statement of former Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Trust General Secretary Champat Rai in the ongoing investigation into alleged embezzlement of Ayodhya temple donations. The Trust has received resignations from Rai and another official.

articles.title: Ram Mandir donation theft: Strict action assured, accused in court
articles.description: UP Deputy CM Brajesh Pathak assured an impartial probe and strict action in the Ram Mandir donation theft case. This comes as eight accused appeared in court and the local bar association deliberates on providing them with legal representation.

articles.title: Thousands of US immigrants got scammed by an attorney exploiting humanitarian visas, lawsuits say
articles.description: A Washington attorney, Alexandra Lozano, is accused of fabricating abuse and trafficking claims for tens of thousands of immigrants seeking legal status. Her firm allegedly created fake stories, drained clients' finances, and left them facing deportation. Lozano surrendered her law license amid lawsuits and investigations, with her firm now closed. This alleged scheme highlights a sharp rise in immigration scams, impacting legitimate victims.

articles.title: Temporary employees can get pension even without formal regularisation if eligible, rules the Supreme Court
articles.description: In a significant ruling, the Supreme Court has declared that temporary-status employees, even without formal regularization, are entitled to pensionary benefits after retirement. This landmark decision, stemming from a case involving postal workers, emphasizes that long service and eligibility under specific rules grant these rights. The court directed authorities to disburse pensions and retiral benefits promptly, asserting that pension is a deferred wage and a constitutional right.

articles.title: Iran's invite to PM Modi for Khamenei's funeral a 'good gesture'
articles.description: Former diplomat Vidya Bhushan Soni terms Iran's funeral invite to PM Modi a 'good gesture,' reflecting India's continued importance as a strategic partner for Tehran despite recent geopolitical complexities and diplomatic gaps.

articles.title: Delhi HC asks CAT to re-examine plea challenging Forest Service exam answer key
articles.description: The Delhi High Court has directed the Central Administrative Tribunal (CAT) to re-examine a Forest Service exam candidate's challenge to the official answer key. The court clarified that questioning incorrect suggested answers is not the same as seeking re-evaluation. It emphasized that courts can intervene if answers are demonstrably wrong, remitting the case for a swift decision on the petitioner's candidature.

articles.title: Bidadi farmers launch ‘Appiko’ drive to denounce township project
articles.description: Farmers protesting against the Bidadi Township Project on Sunday intensified their opposition by launching an “Appiko Drive”, on the lines of the Chipko Movement, to highlight the loss of green cover that the project is expected to cause.
